title: PutItem
text: PutItem Builds a list of Name=Value pairs. int PutItem(unsigned char * List , unsigned char * Name , unsigned char * Value , unsigned char ** Next ) Parameters Type Name Direction Description unsigned char * List IN List to build unsigned char * Name IN Name of item unsigned char * Value IN Value of item unsigned char ** Next OUT Pointer to next location; *next should be zero on the first call. The caller should not change the value. Return Values Value Description >0 Length of list Description This function builds a null-terminated list of items, in which the items are separated by a byte value of 27, 0x1b. Items are pairs in the form Name=Value . Given the name and value, the function returns a pointer to the next location to be used and the total length of the list. The function inserts the separator if necessary. The list names and values may be in UTF-8. Separators such as = are in ASCII. In this example, PutItem is being used to store the character set used: Explain this code /* Was Unicode or OEM negotiated ? */ if (DrvArea->NegFlags & FLG_NEGOTIATE_UNICODE) PutItem(SecDrv->OutBuf, (USTRING*)"CHARSET", (USTRING*)"UNICODE", &ptr); else PutItem(SecDrv->OutBuf, (USTRING*)"CHARSET", (USTRING*)"OEM", &ptr); Copy Was this topic helpful?
